title: Expert in setting up Content Creation, scheduling and process board
excerpt: We are looking to hire an expert to set up and optimise a number of boards for our content creation, content scheduling and other relevant content information boards so that we can store, create & schedule everything fro…

posts.author: Caterina (Caterina Ruberto)
posts.cookedHtml: We are looking to hire an expert to set up and optimise a number of boards for our content creation, content scheduling and other relevant content information boards so that we can store, create & schedule everything from RAW material through to final copy of content for splaying over multiple platforms.

posts.author: timlittletech (Tim Little)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Caterina I would be happy to sit down and chat with you through the content creation, scheduling and process board. I have worked with many agencies and setup similar templates as what it sounds like you are looking to setup. I’ll send you a direct message too in relation to the CRM processes that you are looking to utilize monday.com for.

Please book a time here if you are interested in discussing more! Calendly - Tim Little

Best!

posts.author: MyManMonday.com (Gideon Harris)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Caterina!

I’ve built several Monday.com workspaces that do exactly this!

I would love to show you what I put together and hear more about your specific needs - you can find more about what I do with monday at MyManMonday.com

A quick overlook of one of the systems I built was for a ghost writing company where I put together a setup board where you can easily add new clients and recurring items that get created on the team board. For example, lets say client X wants a new blog post every week, you add it into the setup board, and each week a new item gets created on the team board and it notifies the appropriate writers giving them all the relevant information to begin right away.

There are many other features it has too, but that’s the primary job and it’s been running flawlessly for 4 months now!

posts.cookedHtml:

Feel free to email me at biancajclement@gmail.com if you’re still seeking assistance.

posts.author: Trackdatr (Mike)
posts.cookedHtml: I totally get how challenging it can be to streamline everything from raw material to the final copy.

posts.author: Trackdatr (Mike)
posts.cookedHtml: One thing I’ve found super helpful is using a suite of AI tools alongside integrated SEO features to speed up the process and maintain quality. For example, I’ve been trying out Creaitor—it’s been a game-changer for optimizing content before scheduling it. It’s great for generating ideas, tweaking drafts, and ensuring everything is SEO-friendly without a ton of extra effort. Just my two cents, but I think AI is really the future of simplifying content workflows!

posts.author: adamwill (Adam Will)
posts.cookedHtml: Looking to optimize your workflow? A professional ghostwriting agency handles content setup, scheduling, and process boards so you can focus on big-picture goals.

posts.author: smithjohn (smith)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi I really like the way you are thinking about setting up a content creation and scheduling board it makes such a difference when everything is organized in one place. One thing that can help a lot is breaking the workflow into clear stages like ideation creation review and publishing so everyone knows what they are responsible for at each step. I have seen this work well for teams handling projects like bostonlimotravel where having a clear visual process reduced confusion and made deadlines easier to track. Do you currently use any kind of automation or reminders to make sure content moves smoothly through the board?
